title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 July 2024.

items.title: **EA** is "sunsetting" three last-gen **Battlefield **games
bullets: Battlefield 3 (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3), Battlefield 4 (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3), Battlefield: Hardline (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3)
On Wednesday, July 31st they will be removed from digital storefronts and you will no longer be able to purchase them and all extra related content such as DLC
Battlefield 3 will still be available for play on PC. Battlefield 4, and Battlefield: Hardline will still be available for play on PC, Xbox One, and PlayStation 4.

items.title: **Dungeons & Dragons** rules update includes changes prompted by game's lead designer playing Baldur's Gate 3
**PlayStation Stars** loyalty scheme returning after a month of unavailability
bullets: The service, which allows you to change points you've earnt for prizes, such as virtual collectables or PSN vouchers, has been down since early June
It's being rolled out again in phased, regional rollouts. Specific times are unknown.

items.title: **Nintendo **sues Switch modchip seller and Switch piracy Reddit moderator
bullets: The company has filed complaints against Ryan Daly, who operates a store called Modded Hardware, and r/SwitchPirates moderator Archbox, whose real name is James Williams.
In addition to being a moderator on Reddit, Nintendo claims Archbox has been involved with three "pirate shops" (which have since been closed) and cliams that he's involved with a fourth active one.

items.title: **MultiVersus **update adds Agent Smith, free-for-all and spectator mode
bullets: Version 1.05 also makes rifts easier and rebalances fighters.

items.title: July’s **PlayStation Plus Essential** games are now live
bullets: This month’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are Borderlands 3 (PS4/5), NHL 24 (PS4/5) and Among Us (PS4/5).
Members have until August 6 to add these titles to their game library.
Subscribers will also be able to claim the exclusive Genshin Impact PlayStation Plus Pack starting on July 16

bullets.text: Journey to the Savage Planet (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 3

items.title: **Tales of Kenzera: Zau** studio is hit with layoffs ("Just over a dozen")
bullets: Tales of Kenzera: Zau was released earlier this year. Published as an EA Original title, the game received generally positive reviews at release, with a Metacritic score of 76.

items.title: More **Xbox **Layoffs happening / happened this week.
bullets: One employee had their role eliminated whilst on vacation
